title: The Twenty-First Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 25, 2020 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Twenty-First Sunday after Pentecost in the liturgical year (Year A in the Revised Common Lectionary). The readings would have been Exodus 22:1-15 (or 1-9), Psalm 107:1-9, 43, 1 Thessalonians 1:1-10, and Matthew 22:15-22—the famous passage about rendering to Caesar and to God. The church was in Ordinary Time (also called Kingdomtide), the long green season between Pentecost and Advent, which had begun in late May 2020.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the period following its General Convention, which had been postponed from summer 2020 due to the pandemic and would eventually occur in July 2021. Diocesan life in the Diocese of Colorado (which includes Saint John's Cathedral) was deeply affected by COVID-19 protocols—worship was limited capacity or hybrid in-person/online. No major bishop elections or significant denominational controversies were immediate news at this specific moment, though pandemic reopening decisions were contentious across dioceses.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Colorado was in the final stretch before the November 3 general election. Denver's Capitol Hill neighborhood (where the Cathedral is located) remained under various COVID-19 restrictions, with downtown foot traffic significantly reduced from normal autumn levels. The week before this sermon, Colorado was experiencing unseasonably warm fall weather in the high 70s to 80s Fahrenheit, unusual for late October.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The 2020 presidential campaign was in its final two weeks, with Donald Trump and Joe Biden in the homestretch. COVID-19 cases were surging nationally again, particularly in the Midwest and parts of the South. The Senate was days away from the Amy Coney Barrett confirmation vote (confirmed October 26). The New York Times had just published Trump's tax return reporting (published October 2, still major news). The Trump administration was being criticized for its pandemic response as deaths and cases climbed.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The United Kingdom was experiencing a second COVID-19 wave and would enter a new tiered restriction system within days. The U.S.-Mexico border remained a site of significant immigration enforcement and controversy. The pandemic was causing economic disruption globally, with many countries in various stages of lockdown or reopening.

CULTURAL MOMENT

America was deeply polarized and anxious heading into the final election days. The country was simultaneously in month seven of the COVID-19 pandemic and month five since the George Floyd protests, though that movement had receded from constant headlines. Masks and pandemic protocols had become partisan symbols. There was widespread election anxiety and concern about potential civil unrest. Many Americans were voting early by mail for the first time. The cultural moment was characterized by deep dread, polarization, and uncertainty about the nation's direction.
